title: AG: Meadows won’t face voter fraud charges in North Carolina
shortDescription: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Mark Meadows, the former White House chief of staff to President Donald Trump, won't face voter fraud charges related to his 2020 registration and absentee vote in North Carolina, the state's attorney general announced Friday.

title: Trump’s taxes: Takeaways from release of long-sought returns
shortDescription: In one of its last acts under Democratic control, the House of Representatives on Friday released six years of former President Donald Trump's tax returns, dating to 2015, the year he announced his presidential bid.

title: Ginni Thomas says she regrets post-election texts to Meadows
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Virginia Thomas, the wife of U.S. Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas, says she regrets sending texts to then-White House chief of staff Mark Meadows after the 2020 election, telling the House Jan.

title: Trump’s returns shed light on tax offsets, foreign accounts
Biden pardons 6 convicted of murder, drug, alcohol crimes
shortDescription: KINGSHILL, U.S. Virgin Islands (AP) — President Joe Biden has pardoned six people who have served out sentences after convictions on a murder charge and drug- and alcohol-related crimes, including an 80-year-old woman convicted of killing her abusive husband about a half-century ago and a man who pleaded guilty to using a telephone for a cocaine transaction in the 1970s.

title: Jan. 6 takeaways: Final revelations from investigation
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Destroyed documents. Suggestions of pardoning violent rioters. Quiet talks among cabinet officials about whether then-President Donald Trump should be removed from office. Interview transcripts released by House investigators in recent days — more than 100 so far — give further insight into the Jan.

title: Afghan refugees in US face uncertainty as legislation stalls
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congress has failed so far to create a path to residency for Afghans who worked alongside U.S.

title: ‘You’ve got to deliver’: Democrats take charge in Michigan
shortDescription: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Democrats will be in charge of Michigan's state government for the first time in nearly 40 years come January, raising progressive hopes of undoing decades of Republican-backed measures and advancing an agenda that includes restrictions on guns and help for the working poor.

title: Biden makes 1st public appearances during St. Croix vacation
shortDescription: CHRISTIANSTED, U.S. Virgin Islands (AP) — After two days out of sight while vacationing in the U.S. Virgin Islands, President Joe Biden ventured out Friday for a round of golf and dinner with his wife and grandchildren.

title: Time of triumph for GOP turns into ‘distraction’ with Santos
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — It should be a time of triumph for Republicans ready to take back control of the House in the new Congress next week, but their leaders are struggling with an embarrassing distraction about one of their own: What to do about George Santos?

title: Court: Abortion doctors can’t be charged under Arizona law
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — An Arizona court has ruled that abortion doctors cannot be prosecuted under a pre-statehood law that criminalizes nearly all abortions yet was barred from being enforced for decades.

title: N. Carolina doesn’t pick electric mix to lower carbon levels
shortDescription: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina utility regulators told Duke Energy Corp. on Friday to carry out a series of activities to generate electricity that they say will help ensure greenhouse gas reductions set in a new state law are met.

title: Venezuelan opposition strips Guaidó of ‘presidential’ role
shortDescription: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — For three years, Juan Guaidó led the Venezuelan opposition’s efforts to bring about new elections and remove socialist President Nicolás Maduro.But on Friday, dozens of politicians who once backed Guaidó voted in favor of removing the 39-year-old engineer and replacing his U.S.-supported “interim government” with a committee to oversee presidential primaries next year and protect the nation’s assets abroad.

title: FEC levies $30K penalty tied to Kobach’s 2020 US Senate bid
shortDescription: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Incoming Kansas Attorney General Kris Kobach agreed to help pay a $30,000 penalty to resolve a federal complaint over the improper use of a group's email list during his unsuccessful 2020 campaign for the U.S.

title: Candidates sign up for Jan. 31 races for state House, Senate
shortDescription: ATLANTA (AP) — Three candidates have signed up for a Jan. 31 special election to fill a vacant state Senate seat in southwest Georgia, while a Republican county administrator will run unopposed for an open seat in the state House.

title: Queen guitarist, women’s soccer team top UK honors list
shortDescription: LONDON (AP) — Those at the forefront of the U.K.'s response to Russia's war in Ukraine joined Queen guitarist Brian May and a fashion designer dubbed “the mother of the miniskirt” on the country’s New Year's Honors list on Friday.

title: Attorney General files intent to appeal judge’s bail ruling
shortDescription: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — Illinois Attorney General Kwame Raoul on Friday notified the state Supreme Court that he will appeal a local judge's ruling that eliminating cash bail for criminal defendants is unconstitutional.

title: Alabama to allow concealed guns without permit in 2023
shortDescription: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama on Jan. 1 will become the latest state to allow people to carry a concealed handgun without a state permit that requires a background check.The new state law ends the requirement for a person to get a permit to legally carry a concealed handgun in public.

title: Wisconsin ‘committee’ wagers on elections over fish, beer
shortDescription: LA CROSSE, Wis. (AP) — Former politicians and political experts in western Wisconsin are putting their money where their mouths are when it comes to elections.The group, which touts itself as the Select Committee on Election Predictions, has been meeting for nearly 50 years in the La Crosse area to bet $5 on local, state and national elections, Wisconsin Public Radio reported.

title: EPA finalizes water rule that repeals Trump-era changes
shortDescription: ST. LOUIS (AP) — President Joe Biden’s administration on Friday finalized regulations that protect hundreds of thousands of small streams, wetlands and other waterways, repealing a Trump-era rule that federal courts had thrown out and that environmentalists said left waterways vulnerable to pollution.

title: Putin, Xi vow closer ties as Russia bombards Ukraine again
shortDescription: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin and Chinese leader Xi Jinping vowed Friday to deepen their bilateral cooperation against the backdrop of Moscow’s 10-month war in Ukraine, which weathered another night of drone and rocket attacks following a large-scale missile bombardment.

title: Top takeaways from Donald Trump's tax returns
shortDescription: Six years of former President Donald Trump's tax returns have been released by Congress after an eight-year legal battle.

shortDescription: The release contained six years of tax returns over the time Donald Trump was president and campaigned for the White House, from 2015 to 2020.

title: Read the documents: Donald Trump's tax returns released
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump's personal and business tax returns over six years include thousands of pages. Read a copy of Trump's tax returns here.

title: On tropical vacation, Biden mulls: To be or not to be (a 2024 candidate)?
shortDescription: President Joe Biden, on new year's holiday in St. Croix, says he plans to run for the White House but wants to talk a 2024 campaign over with family.

title: Biden signs $1.7 trillion bill funding government operations
shortDescription: President Joe Biden signed a $1.7 trillion spending bill to keep the federal government operating through federal budget year-end in September 2023.

title: Ex-Transportation secretary Elaine Chao addresses Trump's "racist taunt"
shortDescription: Chao and her husband, Republican Kentucky Sen. Mitch McConnell, typically stay mum about former President Donald Trump's inflammatory comments.

shortDescription: Trump's tax returns from 2015-2020 are expected to be released Friday after a House panel vote. What to know

title: 118th Congress to be sworn in, both parties will have slim majorities with split control
shortDescription: Without full Democratic control in both chambers, the Biden administration could face some uphill battles, like Republican legislation and possible investigations.

title: Arizona Republican Kari Lake appeals election challenge loss
shortDescription: Failed GOP Arizona gubernatorial candidate Lake is appealing her election challenge loss and a decision ordering her to pay Katie Hobbs' legal fees.

title: Trump tax returns will be released Friday, House panel says
shortDescription: A U.S. House committee is planning to release six years of former President Donald Trump’s tax returns Friday, a spokesperson confirmed to USA TODAY.

title: SCOTUS blocks Biden's ending Title 42
shortDescription: The policy, which has been in place since the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic, must continue while courts assess a lawsuit filed by 19 states.

title: Supreme Court blocks Biden from ending Title 42 migrant expulsions
shortDescription: The policy, which has been in place since the early days of the pandemic, must continue while courts assess a lawsuit filed by 19 states.

title: What's on voters' presidential wish list?
shortDescription: The leadership style, age, gender, experience and where their ideal candidate would call home, revealed in the latest exclusive poll.

title: Biden to hold airlines accountable after flight cancellations
shortDescription: About 4,000 domestic U.S. flights were canceled Monday, according to the tracking website FlightAware, which tracks flight status in real time.

title: Biden to spend New Year's holiday in U.S. Virgin Islands
shortDescription: Joe Biden will become the first sitting president to visit the U.S. Virgin Islands since Harry Truman delivered a speech there in 1948.

title: NY Rep.-elect Santos admits lying about career during election bid
shortDescription: Rep.-elect George Santos, R-N.Y., admitted that he lied about his credentials during his successful campaign for a seat in the U.S. House.

title: GOP stumbles with independents contributed to midterm election outcome
shortDescription: Republican House candidates nationwide won the support of 38% of independent voters in last month’s midterm elections, according to AP VoteCast.

title: El Paso city leaders call on Biden to visit the border amidst crisis
shortDescription: The mayor said emergency measure is "a band-aid on a broken system" and some council members say President Biden needs to see the crisis in El Paso.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: The IRS Hasn’t Released Nearly Half a Million Nonprofit Tax Records
shortDescription: If you’re trying to evaluate a charity this year, you might have a hard time. The IRS is behind on releasing Form 990s, limiting access to key financial information the public uses to evaluate the nation’s tax-exempt companies.

title: For Democrats, Spending Package Marked One Final Opportunity
shortDescription: For all their accomplishments in the past two years, Democrats face a far tougher environment to see most of their priorities through.By Emily Cochrane

title: How the Speaker’s Election Could Devolve Into Chaos
shortDescription: Representative Kevin McCarthy, the Republican leader, has pledged to take his fight for the speakership to the House floor. It could turn into a debacle not seen in the chamber for a century.By Catie Edmondson

title: Democrats Outpace Trump in Seating Federal Judges, With 97 Confirmed
shortDescription: The Senate’s top two Democrats say they will continue their push to fill vacancies in 2023, but activists warn that under current rules, it will be hard to keep up the pace.By Carl Hulse

title: Advice From a Political Daughter: ‘Every Woman Needs a Paul Pelosi’
shortDescription: Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s husband, a multimillionaire venture capitalist recovering from a brutal attack, has long taken care of the couple’s “business of living,” including shopping for the speaker’s clothes.By Annie Karni

title: McCarthy struggles to appease conservative demands as speakership battle nears
shortDescription: The GOP leader again raised making it easier for members to force a vote to depose the speaker, a move that could severely weaken him if he does manage to take the gavel next term.
